text:
## Team Context

**Your assigned name**: spec-architect-frontend
**Team Lead**: team-lead (use SendMessage to reach me)

**Communication Protocol** (you have SendMessage in your tools):
- `QUESTION: ...` -- before starting, if requirements have genuine ambiguity
- `BLOCKER: ...` -- immediately if you cannot proceed
- `DONE: {summary}` -- when your deliverables are complete
- `SUGGESTION: ...` -- proactively flag tech issues, arch concerns, refactoring ideas

**Context Strategy**: repomix
All relevant context is pre-loaded below. Use Read/Glob/Grep for additional files.

**Escalation rule**: Do NOT work silently on ambiguity. Ask first.

---

## Task

You are the SENIOR FRONTEND ARCHITECT for Notesaner. Your job is to create a deep, production-ready frontend architecture specification covering every page, component, state management pattern, and UX flow.

### Read These Files First
- `/Users/zeizel/projects/notesaner/docs/architecture/overview.md`
- `/Users/zeizel/projects/notesaner/docs/requirements.md`
- `/Users/zeizel/projects/notesaner/docs/proposed-features.md`
- `/Users/zeizel/projects/notesaner/docs/tech-stack.md`
- `/Users/zeizel/projects/notesaner/libs/contracts/src/` -- all files
- `/Users/zeizel/projects/notesaner/research/note-app-pain-points-report.md`

### Deliverables

Write a COMPREHENSIVE frontend architecture document to:
`/Users/zeizel/projects/notesaner/docs/artifacts/wave-01-specs/02-frontend-architecture.md`

This document MUST cover:

#### 1. Feature-Sliced Design (FSD) Structure
- Complete directory tree for apps/web/src/
- Layer definitions: app, pages, widgets, features, entities, shared
- What goes in each layer, naming conventions
- Cross-cutting concerns (theme, i18n, auth)

#### 2. Next.js App Router Pages
For EVERY page/route:
- Route path
- Layout nesting
- Data loading strategy (RSC vs client)
- Auth requirements
- SEO metadata
- List ALL pages:
  - `/login`, `/register`, `/sso/[provider]`
  - `/` (workspace dashboard)
  - `/w/[workspaceId]` (main editor workspace)
  - `/w/[workspaceId]/settings` (workspace settings)
  - `/w/[workspaceId]/graph` (full graph view)
  - `/w/[workspaceId]/admin` (admin panel)
  - `/public/[slug]` (published vault)
  - `/public/[slug]/[...notePath]` (published note)
  - `/settings` (user settings)
  - `/onboarding` (first-run wizard)
  - `/plugins` (plugin browser)

#### 3. Component Architecture
For EVERY major component:
- Component name and path
- Props interface
- State management (local vs Zustand vs TanStack Query)
- Child components
- Events/callbacks
- Accessibility considerations

Major components to specify:
- **EditorShell** (main workspace layout)
- **NoteEditor** (TipTap editor wrapper)
- **FileExplorer** (tree view with drag-and-drop)
- **TabBar** (closable, reorderable tabs)
- **SplitPane** (resizable split with dnd-kit)
- **CommandPalette** (Cmd+P)
- **QuickSwitcher** (Cmd+O)
- **SearchPanel** (global search with results)
- **BacklinksPanel** (right sidebar)
- **OutlinePanel** (right sidebar)
- **GraphView** (d3-force + WebGL)
- **PublishConfig** (publishing settings)
- **SettingsPage** (user/workspace settings)
- **AdminPanel** (user management, providers)
- **PluginBrowser** (discover and install plugins)
- **CommentThread** (inline comments)
- **OnboardingWizard** (first-run experience)
- Every shadcn/ui component needed in packages/ui

#### 4. State Management Architecture
- Zustand store structure (which stores, what they hold)
- TanStack Query patterns (query keys, cache strategy, optimistic updates)
- Local component state patterns
- URL state (search params)
- Define EVERY Zustand store:
  - workspaceStore
  - editorStore
  - sidebarStore
  - tabStore
  - layoutStore
  - themeStore
  - searchStore
  - pluginStore
  - presenceStore
  - settingsStore

#### 5. TipTap Editor Architecture
- Extension registration system
- Custom node types (wiki link, callout, code block, math, etc.)
- Custom marks
- Toolbar configuration
- Slash command menu implementation
- Markdown serializer architecture
- Collaborative editing integration (Yjs)
- Editor modes (WYSIWYG, Live Preview, Source)

#### 6. Real-Time Collaboration UI
- Yjs provider setup (y-websocket)
- Cursor rendering (user colors, avatars)
- Presence indicators
- Selection awareness rendering
- Offline indicator
- Sync status indicator
- Connection state management

#### 7. Window Management System
- dnd-kit integration architecture
- Panel tree data structure
- Split/merge/resize operations
- Snap layout engine
- Layout serialization/deserialization
- Floating window system
- Keyboard shortcuts for window management

#### 8. Theme System
- CSS custom properties structure
- Dark/light mode implementation
- Theme token categories (colors, spacing, typography, shadows)
- shadcn/ui theme integration
- User-customizable themes
- Plugin theme injection

#### 9. i18n Architecture
- next-intl setup
- Message file structure
- String extraction workflow
- RTL support considerations
- Date/number formatting

#### 10. Performance Architecture
- Code splitting strategy (per-feature dynamic imports)
- Lazy loading patterns (editor, graph, plugins)
- Virtual scrolling (for file tree, search results, long notes)
- Image optimization (next/image)
- Bundle size budgets per route
- Web Vitals targets (LCP, FID, CLS)

#### 11. Accessibility Architecture
- WCAG 2.1 AA compliance plan
- Keyboard navigation map
- Screen reader strategy
- Focus management
- ARIA patterns for custom components
- Color contrast requirements

#### 12. Component Library Design (packages/ui)
- Complete list of shadcn/ui components to include
- Custom components needed beyond shadcn/ui
- Component API conventions
- Storybook setup plan

#### 13. Open-Source Libraries to Leverage
- React/Next.js ecosystem packages that save building from scratch
- TipTap extensions that already exist
- D3/graph visualization libraries
- dnd-kit patterns/examples to reference
- UI component libraries to borrow from

Send me DONE when complete with: document path and key architectural decisions made.
summary: Spawn frontend architect for component/state specs
